headline: Educators converge on agentic coding orchestration and the security scaffolding that production agent systems now require.
body: The recommended channels center on orchestrating and governing coding agents: Cole Medin demos Omnigent, an open-source meta-harness that routes Claude Code, Codex, and other models as interchangeable workers with human-in-the-loop governance and cross-device sessions [3], while Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 prompt engineering and agentic loops for SaaS, weighing closed versus open models [1], and IBM Technology frames AI pair programming as an assistant embedded across the development lifecycle [4]. Security is the parallel thread: IBM examines whether agents should autonomously detect and reset compromised passwords amid a 200-plus CVE Patch Tuesday [0], and Kagenti applies identity-based security, delegation chains, and zero-trust architecture to multi-agent systems [2]. Beyond the inner circle, OpenRouter's Fusion sends one prompt to multiple models and synthesizes a combined answer near Fable 5 quality at roughly half the cost [11], MiniMax M3 ships open weights with a one-million-token context window and Anthropic and OpenAI API compatibility [8], and Bright Data's talk exposes web agents that claim to search but silently fall back to stale training data behind anti-bot blocks and CAPTCHAs [6]. Anthropic separately showcased bank deployments of Claude in a financial services briefing [13].
